old_claim.note: Early Symbolic Communication is contextual infrastructure or shared knowledge, not a strict hard prerequisite.

new_claim.note: Qafzeh links intentional burial to red ocher near human remains, making pigment practice documented symbolic context rather than a universal hard prerequisite.

source_shape.source_locator: Intentional burial section: as many as 15 Homo sapiens individuals at Qafzeh were found with 71 pieces of red ocher and ocher-stained stone tools; ocher near the bones suggests ritual use.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The Smithsonian source ties the Qafzeh intentional burial evidence to red ocher and ritual interpretation.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports pigment practice as the documented symbolic context for the scoped burial evidence while avoiding a broader unsourced dependency on later symbolic communication categories.

ontology_before: Ritual Burial Practices depended on a broad Early Symbolic Communication node dated much later than the Qafzeh evidence.
ontology_after: Ritual Burial Practices is scoped to Qafzeh-style intentional mortuary treatment with pigment as documented symbolic context.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: the direct early_symbolic_communication prerequisite is replaced by a source-backed pigment_creation common-dependency edge.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped to Upper Paleolithic cave-art communication instead of mortuary treatment.
A source showed Qafzeh burial ritual depended on a separate symbolic communication system rather than contextual pigment use.
The pigment_creation node were narrowed away from ocher-rich pigment practices.
short_reason: Pigment context is source-backed for Qafzeh burial; broad symbolic communication was temporally and semantically too loose.
